rulings.Case Title: IRINA SIGNAEVICH et al VS. THOMAS JEONG GEE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Set Aside And Vacate Entry Of Default Under C.C.P. 473.5
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, April 22, 2015, Line 1, DEFENDANT EDWIN GEE'S Motion To Set Aside And Vacate Entry Of Default Under C.C.P. 473.5. Granted. The default is set aside pursuant to CCP sec. 473.5. Defendant shall file his proposed answer within 10 days of entry of this order. The evidence establishes that Defendant did not receive actual notice of the suit until November 2014. (Declaration of Edwin Gee, par. 7.) The motion is timely filed, as it was filed within 2 years of the default being entered. Although Plaintiffs state that they mailed the notice of default to Defendant, there is no proof of service in the record. Thus, the 2 year deadline applies. Further, there is a strong policy in favor of having cases decided on the merits. Plaintiffs cannot establish prejudice by the default being set aside, as the case is still in the early stages. No trial date has been set and the first substantive motion (a demurrer) is set to be heard on April 23, 2015. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR DETERMINATION OF GOOD FAITH SETTLEMENT, ETC.

rulings.Case Title: NHAN THI PHAN VS. MICHAEL DANG, DBA AS BLUE SKY SPA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order That Genuineness Of Documents Be Deemed Admitted And For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $1860.00
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, April 22, 2015, Line 2, DEFENDANT MICHAEL DANG, DBA AS BLUE SKY SPA's Motion For An Order That Genuineness Of Documents Be Deemed Admitted And For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $1860.00. Pro Tem Judge Peter Catalanotti, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Motion for an Order that the Genuineness of Documents Be Deemed Admitted and for Sanctions is DENIED. Where responses have been timely filed but are deemed deficient by the requesting party (e.g., because of objections or evasive responses), that party may move for an order compelling a further response, not to have the request for genuineness deemed admitted. CCP § 2033.290. The Court treats the Motion as a motion to compel further responses to the RFAS. On that basis, the Motion is GRANTED. Defendant Nhan Thi Phan shall serve complete and straightforward verified responses to each request for genuineness within two weeks of the date of this order. The request for sanctions is DENIED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to pcc@manningllp.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: NINA PINZARRONE VS. STATE OF CALIFORNIA - A SOVEREIGN STATE et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, April 22, 2015, Line 6, DEFENDANT PLACE 2 PARK CORP.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant Place 2 Park Corp.'s demurrers to causes of action 2 and 3 are sustained without leave to amend. Both causes of action rely on a statute/ordinance that only applies to owners of real property and plaintiff alleges that defendant is merely a lessee. (Amended Complaint, pars. 17-21). In addition, negligence per se is an evidentiary presumption, not a distinct claim. See Das v. Bank of America, N.A., (2010) 186 Cal.App.4th 727, 737-738. The court overrules the demurrer to "cause of action" 8 because the cited provision sought to protect pedestrians such as plaintiff. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID BAKER ET AL VS. NINA MACKENZIE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Petitioners Motion To Compel Amended Responses And Responsive Documents To Petiitoners Requests For Production And Motion For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, April 22, 2015, Line 3, DEFENDANT NINA MACKENZIE's Motion To Compel Amended Responses And Responsive Documents To Petitioners' Requests For Production And Motion For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Peter Catalanotti, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Motion to Compel Further Responses is GRANTED and DENIED as follows. The "General Objections and Other Statements" are overruled. See CCP § 2031.240(a). The objection that the RFPs are not reasonably calculated to lead to discovery of admissible evidence and are beyond the scope of permissible discovery is GRANTED in part. The RFPs are limited to documents that refer specifically to Peter McKenzie. The David Baker entities are ordered to provide further responses to the following RFPs: 56, 57, 60, 64, 66, 68, 69, 74, 75, 78, 79, 80, 81, 84, 85, 86, 92, 93, 94, 97-99, 101, 103, 108, 116, 118-119, 143, 147, 156, 157, 170, 192-195. The David Baker entities are ordered to produce documents responsive to these requests within one month of this order together with a privilege log. If the parties agree, the Judge Pro Tem will conduct an in camera review of the documents. The parties are to meet and confer on the appropriate time limits for the RFPs and appear at the hearing with an agreement on an appropriate limit. All requests for sanctions are denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to pcc@manningllp.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: NATIONAL UNION FIRE INSURANCE COMPANY OF PITTSBURG VS. HELEN NGUYEN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Compelling Arbitration And Motion To Stay The Proceedings
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, April 22, 2015, Line 10, DEFENDANT HELEN NGUYEN'S Petition For Order Compelling Arbitration And Motion To Stay The Proceedings. Denied. The Court notes that a First Amended Complaint was filed after the Petition to Compel Arbitration was filed. Because the First Amended Complaint is substantially similar to the original complaint, the Court will rule on the merits of the Petition, rather than take the Petition off calendar as moot. The Arbitration Agreement, read as a whole, only applies to disputes relating to the terms and conditions of Defendant's employment. Plaintiff's causes of action for fraud, unjust enrichment, conversion, and alter ego do not relate to the terms and conditions of Defendant's employment and therefore the Arbitration Agreement does not apply. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
